## [00:00 - 03:05]

In this bonus lesson, I'm going to be taking you through the exact influencer acquisition flow that we use for my brand drop bottle. So this document is really important so that you're able to give it across to your VAs to give them an overall guide of what your campaign is going to be and the actions that they're going to need to take on behalf of your brand. So we want this to be really simply put because English isn't always going to be your VAs first language. So first we have our goal of the campaign which is to get influencers to post about drop bottle. And then we follow up with a couple of our key brand messages. Moving on to our deliverables, we have one feed post and two to three stories and some examples of what stories could include. And then we move on to what to do. So this is just a general overview of the campaign as a whole and what exactly we're looking for from our influencers' behalf. Then we give the VA some reminders. So to use at blank account, which is just an example for your list building account here, this is similar to what we spoke about in our micro-influences at scale lesson. So it's just repeating the steps that we went through in that. So creating the blank account, getting them to use drop bottle as the account to reach out from. So then we have some reminders for our VA. So there's a reminder to use our list building account to find influencers to reach out to. Then about reaching out from the drop bottle account. And if we've been in contact via direct message and they haven't replied, just about sending them a message saying something like, just bumping this up in your inbox. And here we link through your tracking spreadsheet so that your VA is also able to track everything, all of their outreach through this process. So then moving on to our outreach flow. We have our DM to like to comment outreach flow, as well as our emails. And we've put that out into around 10 steps. Then we're moving down into our responses section. So for each of these, we have our copy written for example, for a direct message on Instagram, the sample comments, the following up or bumping up the message in their direct messages, as well as our offer for outreach via email and a confirmation message for once the influencers do a greater collaborate with the brand. We also have our acquisition flow for via email as well. So they're the main messages that we have here. And you might want to also add any other frequently asked questions so that your VA is able to get back to influencers from this point. So moving on from our responses section down into some of our influencer planning.

## [03:05 - 04:40]

So we have influencers across various niches, but ideally lifestyle, beauty, foodies, founders, health and wellness, influencers, etc. We have our targets in terms of 3000 to 10,000 followers, 11,000 to 50,000 followers, 51,000 to 100,000 followers, 100K to 500K and 500K plus, as well as a bit of an expectation from our brands. And in terms of what our projected ROI will be and what our budget is. So for 3 to 10K, it's product and then moving up with some financial compensation as well. And we have some tables with some ideas of influencers that we might want to collaborate with under each tier. So you can see that under the 10K mark, we're putting in their handle, their email, then we'll fill out their name and address, the date that they were sent their product, the date that they posted and whether sales or ROI was generated just from a top plan level, like literally a yes or no answer for each, just so that we can go through later and see at that top plan level, which influencers did or didn't generate sales off this campaign. So that's the influencer acquisition flow in a nutshell. Of course, you're going to have access to this exact document as well. So you can take your time and go through that. So as an action item, you should be filling out one of these for your brand and passing it along to your VA. So go ahead, do that now and I'll pass you along to our next instructor.
